title: Shopping for the Right Fit
learningGoal: The learner will rapidly audibly decode descriptive adjectives within conversational speech, isolating specific modifiers (e.g., fast, big, new) to correctly answer comprehension questions.
instructions: Listen to a customer buying a car and a dog at a store. They have very specific tastes! Pay close attention to the describing words (Adjectives) they use. Does the customer want a BIG car or a SMALL car? Answer all 8 questions.

contentItems.text: Remember, the strongest stress is usually on the adjective. Listen for the pitch of their voice.

listeningGoal: Isolate descriptive adjectives from fluent conversation to extract highly specific item details.
audioScenario: A picky customer trying to decide what to buy at a mixed marketplace.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Hello, I want to buy a car.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: हेलो, आई वांट टू बाय अ कार।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: नमस्ते, मैं एक कार खरीदना चाहता हूँ।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Welcome! We have many cars. Do you want a big car?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: वेलकम! वी हैव मेनी कार्स। डू यू वांट अ बिग कार?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: स्वागत है! हमारे पास कई कारें हैं। क्या आप एक बड़ी कार चाहते हैं?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: No, I do not want a big car. I live in a small house. I need a small car.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: नो, आई डू नॉट वांट अ बिग कार। आई लिव इन अ स्मॉल हाउस। आई नीड अ स्मॉल कार।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: नहीं, मुझे बड़ी कार नहीं चाहिए। मैं एक छोटे घर में रहता हूँ। मुझे एक छोटी कार चाहिए।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: I understand. We have a slow, small car.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: आई अंडरस्टैंड। वी हैव अ स्लो, स्मॉल कार।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: मैं समझता हूँ। हमारे पास एक धीमी, छोटी कार है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: I don't like slow cars! I am a fast driver.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: आई डोंट लाइक स्लो कार्स! आई एम अ फास्ट ड्राइवर।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: मुझे धीमी कारें पसंद नहीं हैं! मैं एक तेज़ ड्राइवर हूँ।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Okay, we have a fast, small car. It is a red car.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ओके, वी हैव अ फास्ट, स्मॉल कार। इट इज़ अ रेड कार।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: ठीक है, हमारे पास एक तेज़, छोटी कार है। यह एक लाल कार है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Perfect. Is it a new car or an old car?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: परफेक्ट। इज़ इट अ न्यू कार और ऐन ओल्ड कार?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: उत्तम। क्या यह एक नई कार है या पुरानी कार है?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: It is an old car, but it is a good car. The price is cheap.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: इट इज़ ऐन ओल्ड कार, बट इट इज़ अ गुड कार। द प्राइस इज़ चीप।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: यह एक पुरानी कार है, लेकिन यह एक अच्छी कार है। कीमत सस्ती है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Good. I will buy it. Also, do you sell dogs?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: गुड। आई विल बाय इट। ऑल्सो, डू यू सेल डॉग्स?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: अच्छा। मैं इसे खरीद लूंगा। इसके अलावा, क्या आप कुत्ते बेचते हैं?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes! We have a happy dog.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस! वी हैव अ हैप्पी डॉग।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ! हमारे पास एक खुश कुत्ता है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Is it a quiet dog or a loud dog?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: इज़ इट अ क्वाइट डॉग और अ लाउड डॉग?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: क्या यह एक शांत कुत्ता है या शोर करने वाला कुत्ता है?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: It is a very loud dog.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: इट इज़ अ वेरी लाउड डॉग।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: यह बहुत शोर करने वाला कुत्ता है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Oh, that is bad news. I will only take the car.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ओह, दैट इज़ बैड न्यूज़। आई विल ओनली टेक द कार।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: ओह, यह बुरी खबर है। मैं बस कार लूंगा।

audioTranscriptFull:
Customer: Hello, I want to buy a car.
Salesman: Welcome! We have many cars. Do you want a big car?
Customer: No, I do not want a big car. I live in a small house. I need a small car.
Salesman: I understand. We have a slow, small car.
Customer: I don't like slow cars! I am a fast driver.
Salesman: Okay, we have a fast, small car. It is a red car.
Customer: Perfect. Is it a new car or an old car?
Salesman: It is an old car, but it is a good car. The price is cheap.
Customer: Good. I will buy it. Also, do you sell dogs?
Salesman: Yes! We have a happy dog.
Customer: Is it a quiet dog or a loud dog?
Salesman: It is a very loud dog.
Customer: Oh, that is bad news. I will only take the car.

questionSet.questionText: What size of car does the customer originally say they DO NOT want?
Why does the customer want a smaller car?
options: Because he is a small man
Because he lives in a small house

questionSet.questionText: What type of driver is the customer?
Is the car they finally agree on new or old?
What color is the fast, small car?
What kind of dog does the salesman offer?
Is the dog a quiet dog or a loud dog?
True or False: The customer says the loud dog is 'bad news'.

scoringRules.partialCreditRules: No partial credit for Q4, Q8.

correctAnswers.q2: Because he lives in a small house

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You were able to track multiple adjectives at conversational speed and map them perfectly to their nouns.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Ensure you playback the audio on Slow mode. Listen specifically to the words describing the car (Big/Small, Fast/Slow).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good ear! Watch out for the dog section. The salesman said it was a HAPPY dog, but the customer didn't want it because it was a LOUD dog.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Customer: Hello, I want to buy a car... (matches full transcript)

hintText: When the customer describes themselves, listen for the 'am' verb: 'I AM a fast driver.'
commonMistakes: Thinking the customer bought the car because it was NEW.
